source: Official Path of Exile wiki and official forum references

Definition.text: Scorch is an Elemental Ailment associated with Fire that lowers affected targets' Elemental Resistances.

Purpose.text: Provide a fire-hit based mitigation pressure channel by reducing target Elemental Resistance values after elemental hit application.

Core Rules.text: Scorch magnitude scales from Fire Damage of a Hit, is capped, and applies reduced resistance for a fixed duration.

Internal Rules.text: Scorch uses the non-damaging elemental ailment pathway and is handled as a separate state from Ignite.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Hit resolves fire damage 2) compute Scorch magnitude versus ailment threshold 3) apply non-damaging ailment effect scaling 4) clamp to maximum 30% reduction 5) apply remaining duration.

Priority Rules.text: Specific Scorch effect applies under the same elemental ailment resistance-reduction channel; resistance-reduction reductions on the same target follow normal effect stacking semantics.

Preconditions.text: A qualifying Fire Hit must occur and the target must be eligible for elemental non-damaging ailments.

Postconditions.text: Target gains a Scorch ailment with reduced Elemental Resistance at Hit resolution until duration expiry.

Limitations.text: Non-damaging ailment avoidance and minimum/maximum effect protections may block or cap Scorch.

Caps.text: Documented current upper cap is up to 30% Elemental Resistance reduction.

Formula.text: Public source provides max-cap statement and scaling model description; exact published numeric formula is not fully specified in this record from collected official references.

Conflicts.text: Scorch is distinct from Ignite and does not itself apply burning damage-over-time.

Exceptions.text: Earlier pre-3.9 wording in community references mentions lower pre-patch duration values; present record uses the current 4-second form from PoE Wiki.

Hidden Rules.text: Affected resistance reduction is treated as resistance-type modifier rather than Hit damage modifier.

Developer Notes.text: Treat Scorch as a non-damaging alternate ailment in this schema with explicit versioned caps and thresholds.

Historical Changes.text: Scorch was introduced as part of alternate ailment expansion in 3.5 and later maintained as a lower-cap alternate elemental ailment branch.

text.note: Scorch introduced as alternate elemental ailment.
Alternate ailments including Scorch documented with lower-cap behavior versus classic non-damaging elemental ailments.

text: A high Fire Hit can apply strong Scorch up to the resistance reduction cap for 4 seconds.
Scorch is not Ignite; it does not add burn damage over time.
Scorch reduction is a resistance effect, not Hit damage modification.

modifies: fire and general Elemental damage taken
cannot_affect: purely physical damage channels without Elemental effect path
